items.title: v0.3 Quality Manual Platform
items.desc: Unified tabbed UI for M1-M4, AI-powered SOP intake, draft/approval workflow. Phases 14-16 complete (schema, API, tabbed UI shell, M3 programs UI), 2 phases remaining.

items.title: Procore Drawing Zip Import
items.desc: Extract PDFs from Procore zip exports and route to project discipline folders. Handle revision superseding.

items.title: Batch WPQ/WPS Extraction
items.desc: Extract ~500 welding PDFs from D:\RAW_DATA\ using dual Sonnet + Opus shadow review pipeline.

items.title: Quality Manual Module 3 XML Authoring
items.desc: Author module3_output.xml from planning doc. 8 sections: Program Framework (3.1), Welding Quality (3.2), Training & Workforce (3.3), Testing & Calibration (3.4), CI/CAPA (3.5), Field Operations Manual (3.6), Vendor & Subcontractor Quality (3.7), Internal Audit (3.8).

items.title: Quality Manual Module 4 XML Authoring
items.desc: Author module4_output.xml from planning doc. ~50 SOPs across 11 categories with dual ID system (section 4.X + SOP-XXX-NNN). Reserved section ranges per category.

items.title: v0.4 Phase 24 — Equipment System Model & Consolidation
items.desc: System type taxonomy (20 types), equipment_system_members junction table for multi-system membership, consolidate refrigeration circuits into real systems, create plumbing/HVAC/compressed air systems, assign 77% of unassigned equipment, system view on dashboard.

items.title: v0.4 Phase 25 — Schedule-First Multi-Pass Extraction
items.desc: Schedule-first extraction strategy: (1a) Haiku extracts equipment schedules to build authoritative manifest, (1b) Haiku extracts legends/abbreviations, (2) Sonnet extracts floor plans/P&IDs with schedule-built checklist as context, (3) Haiku cross-discipline informed search, (4) Sonnet conflict verification on ~20-30%. Prevents hallucination by validating plan findings against schedule data.

items.title: v0.4 Phase 26 — R0001 Legend Extraction & Type Refinement
items.desc: Extract P&ID legend from R0001 Rev E via Claude vision, store in drawing_abbreviations table, use as authoritative lookup for instrument/component classification, resolve naming inconsistencies (Control Valve vs Electronic Expansion Valve).

items.title: Submittal Builder (PM/Vendor Tool)
items.desc: Web-based submittal creation tool for PMs and vendors. Pre-populates from schedule_extractions: equipment tags, spec sections, required manufacturers, quantities. Wizard flow: (1) Select project + spec section, (2) System shows required equipment from drawings, (3) Vendor uploads product data sheets/cut sheets per item, (4) System validates completeness (all required items covered, spec section matches), (5) Generates formatted submittal package with standardized naming (project-spec_section-revision), cover sheet, table of contents, and item cross-reference matrix. Enforces consistent structure so downstream submittal intake/review is predictable. Supports resubmittal workflow (track revisions, reviewer comments, approval status). Vendor portal option for external access without full QMS login.

items.title: Submittal Intake & Cross-Reference
items.desc: AI-powered submittal processing: extract manufacturer, model, spec section, approved/rejected status from submittal PDFs. Cross-reference against schedule_extractions to verify submitted equipment matches design specs. Flag mismatches (wrong model, missing submittals, unapproved substitutions). Schema: submittals table + submittal_items with FK to equipment. Builder-generated submittals get fast-tracked (structure already known); external submittals get full AI extraction.

items.title: Specification Intake & Requirements Extraction
items.desc: Parse project specification PDFs by CSI division. Extract: approved manufacturers, material requirements, test/inspection requirements, submittal requirements per section. Link spec sections to equipment types for automated compliance checking. Build on existing sis-spec-intake foundation.

items.title: Quality Documentation Package Generator & Tracker
items.desc: Quality department deliverable to operations: generates pre-filled test and inspection documents for every piece of equipment on the project, organized by system and project stage. Philosophy: QA provides the field with ready-to-execute packages where all the hard parts (equipment tags, spec references, acceptance criteria, design values, related drawing numbers) are already filled in — the field only needs to record actual test results and sign. This removes the documentation burden from operations while ensuring QA gets complete, high-quality data back. Three phases: (1) GENERATE — Cross-reference quality manual M3 programs, project specifications (test/inspection requirements per CSI section), and equipment registry to produce a master documentation matrix. For each equipment tag or system, determine every required document (hydrostatic test, insulation resistance, weld inspection, pressure test, balancing report, startup checklist, etc.) and at which project stage it's needed (pre-install, install, startup, commissioning, closeout). Generate pre-filled PDF/form packages per system with equipment-specific data populated from the drawing database (tag, location, design pressure, design temp, voltage, HP, flow rates, acceptance criteria from specs). (2) TRACK — Web dashboard showing overall project quality documentation status. System-level drill-down: 'Chilled Water System: 14 of 18 documents complete'. Equipment-level: 'CH-1: startup report pending, hydro test complete'. Stage gates: cannot mark system commissioning-ready until all install-stage docs are complete. Supports self-perform QC (SIS crews) and subcontractor document collection with the same pre-filled templates. (3) CLOSE — Completed documents are processed back into the project quality database. System assembles final turnover packages per system for owner delivery: all test reports, inspection records, certifications, calibration certs, O&M manuals, and as-built data in structured format. Turnover package generation is automated — when all documents for a system are marked complete, the package is ready to print/export. The real value: the quality department can hand operations a box of pre-filled documents at project kickoff and say 'fill in the blanks and return these as you go' instead of hoping they figure out what's required from a 500-page spec. Field employees get clear, specific forms. QA gets complete, consistent data. The owner gets a professional turnover package.

items.title: RFI Intake & Tracking
items.desc: Process RFI PDFs: extract question, response, referenced drawings/specs, status, dates, responsible parties. Link RFIs to affected equipment tags and drawing sheets. Track open/closed status, response time metrics. Web UI for RFI log with filters and search.

items.title: Windows Service (nssm)
items.desc: Run Waitress as auto-start Windows service instead of manual qms serve.

items.title: Scheduled OneDrive Sync
items.desc: Auto-run qms pipeline intake --sync on a schedule via Task Scheduler or service.

items.title: P-Number Prefix Disambiguation
items.desc: P- prefix maps to Mechanical but should be Plumbing for some projects. Need project-level override or smarter resolution.

items.title: v0.4 Phase 25 — Schedule-First Extraction (3 plans)

Shadow review caught M6001 75% Docling gap. Source tracking + tiered confidence on all entries.

items.title: v0.4 Phase 24 — System Model & Consolidation
items.desc: System type taxonomy (15 types), equipment_system_members junction table, system builder consolidating 13 RS-* circuits into 5 real refrigeration systems, 5 plumbing systems, 3 mechanical systems. System assignment 23% to 82.8%. Dashboard System|Grouped|All three-mode toggle.

items.desc: Tag parser for parent-child hierarchy, reversed tag normalization, dedup 124 duplicate pairs (589→465), sub-component type reclassification (CV→Control Valve, etc.), grouped dashboard view toggle (Grouped|All) with expandable parent rows.

items.desc: Cross-discipline attribute conflict detection (rule-based with tolerance), missing-discipline negative space scanner, CLI command with summary and RFI output. 492 conflicts detected for Vital (2 attribute, 490 missing discipline).
